questions.prompt: You want to write and compile Java automation, not just run someone else's program. Which do you install?
options: The JVM only
The JRE
The JDK
Just an IDE — no Java needed underneath
questions.explanation: The JDK includes the compiler (javac) and tools needed to develop. The JRE/JVM only run already-compiled code, and an IDE still needs a JDK underneath to compile.

questions.prompt: What is the role of javac versus java?
options: javac compiles .java into .class bytecode; java runs that bytecode on the JVM
javac runs the program; java compiles it
They are two names for the same command
javac downloads libraries; java installs them
questions.explanation: Compilation (javac) and execution (java) are two distinct steps. A compile error happens before the program ever runs.

questions.prompt: The JVM starts a program by looking for which exact method signature?
options: public void start()
static main(args)
public static void main(String[] args)
public main(String args)
questions.explanation: public static void main(String[] args) is the required entry point. The JVM will not start a class that lacks this exact signature.

questions.prompt: Your code has a typo and javac reports an error. What is true?
options: The program ran but produced wrong output
No .class file was produced, so the program never executed
The JVM caught it at runtime
The test passed because main was found
questions.explanation: A compile error stops the build; no bytecode is produced and nothing runs. That is different from a runtime/test failure, which happens during execution.

options: Reads a line from the user
Writes "hi" plus a newline to standard output
Compiles the current class
Saves "hi" to a file named out
questions.explanation: out is the standard output stream and println writes the text followed by a newline. (print would omit the newline.)
